metadata.greeting: Get ready for a fun-filled weekend in London! I've found a great mix of local activities in Hackney and East London, plus some exciting options further afield. There are free museum visits, puppet shows, and outdoor adventures to keep your little one entertained.

activities.title: Young V&A Museum Visit
activities.description: Explore the newly transformed Young V&A in east London, co-designed with children and young people. It is aimed at the interests, skills and needs of children and teenagers as they build the creative confidence needed to thrive in our fast-paced world.
activities.whyItWorks: The museum has three permanent gallery spaces called Imagine, Play and Design. Visitors can get hands-on with interactive displays, workshops and an Open Studio, and show off performance skills on the amphitheatre-style stage in the Imagine area of the museum.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Bethnal Green
transport.tip: The Bethnal Green tube station is not accessible, Whitechapel is the closest accessible tube station which is a 30 minute walk or bus ride away.

activities.title: Museum of London Docklands
activities.description: Discover the history of London's waterfront through interactive exhibits and displays. Explore old warehouses, see historic ships, and learn about the people who worked in the docks. There is also a Soft Play area.
activities.whyItWorks: Designed with children in mind, they offer free activity sheets specially designed for different ages, and so many hands-on activities for them to enjoy as they explore.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Shoreditch High Street, then the DLR to West India Quay.

activities.title: Spitalfields City Farm Visit
activities.description: A hidden gem near Brick Lane with a community feel. Check out their amazing line up of activities.
activities.whyItWorks: A great place to see animals and enjoy a bit of nature in the city. Perfect for a short visit with a toddler.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Shoreditch High Street, then walk to Spitalfields.

activities.title: The Everywhere Bear
activities.description: Adapted from the Julia Donaldson picture book, this show follows the titular teddy on his adventures using rhyme, puppets and new songs.
activities.whyItWorks: The Little Angel Theatre is dedicated to puppetry and live animation in all its diverse forms.

transport.fromHackney: Take the bus 38 or 56 to Essex Road, then walk to the theatre.
transport.tip: Check for Friday Fives scheme makes £5 tickets available at 5pm on Friday.

activities.title: Saturday Puppet Club
activities.description: Little Angel's exciting Saturday morning club for 2-5 year olds and their carers. Members begin the term by watching a performance of 'The Birthday Cake' and meeting the puppets. The following 7 weeks are spent on 'crafty' activities, songs and games linked to the show including some simple puppet making. The children must be accompanied by an adult carer and will leave each session with something they have made themselves.
activities.whyItWorks: This is a really nice opportunity to engage with puppetry through craft activities and songs.

transport.fromHackney: Take the bus 38 or 56 to Essex Road, then walk to the theatre.

activities.title: Victoria Park Visit
activities.description: Enjoy a day out at Victoria Park, one of London's oldest and largest parks.
activities.whyItWorks: There are playgrounds, green spaces, and a lake to explore. Great for running around and enjoying the outdoors.

transport.fromHackney: Walk, cycle, or take a bus to Victoria Park.

activities.title: Hackney City Farm Visit
activities.description: Visit Hackney City Farm to see a variety of farm animals and learn about urban farming.
activities.whyItWorks: A small and friendly farm, perfect for a quick visit with a toddler. They can see chickens, pigs, and other animals up close.

transport.fromHackney: Walk or cycle to Hackney City Farm.

activities.title: Queen Elizabeth Olympic Park
activities.description: Explore the playgrounds, fountains, and green spaces of the Queen Elizabeth Olympic Park.
activities.whyItWorks: Plenty of space for running around, interactive fountains, and playgrounds suitable for toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Stratford.

activities.title: Natural History Museum Visit
activities.description: Explore the wonders of the natural world, from dinosaurs to insects.
activities.whyItWorks: The museum has lots of exhibits that appeal to young children, including the dinosaur gallery and the marine life exhibits.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to Green Park, then the Piccadilly Line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Skip the queue with a free entry ticket.

activities.title: Science Museum Visit
activities.description: Discover how the world works with interactive exhibits on space, technology, and more.
activities.whyItWorks: The Wonderlab offers a great hands-on experience for kids with experiment demonstrations, talks, activities and educational play zones.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to Green Park, then the Piccadilly Line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Book tickets for the Wonderlab for a more interactive experience.

activities.title: V&A Museum Visit
activities.description: Explore 5,000 years of art and design at the world's largest museum of decorative arts.
activities.whyItWorks: There are lots of interesting objects to see, from sculptures to fashion. The museum also offers family activities and workshops.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to Green Park, then the Piccadilly Line to South Kensington.

activities.title: Zoonormous Egg Hunt at London Zoo
activities.description: Explore London Zoo on a special Easter egg hunt, searching for six giant Easter eggs hidden around the park. Each egg reveals a secret letter and a riddle to solve and at the end there's a chocolate prize! In addition there is arts and crafts activities and Easter Bunny crowns.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines a visit to the zoo with an Easter egg hunt. Kids can see animals and enjoy a fun activity.

transport.fromHackney: Take the bus 38 to Angel, then the Northern Line to Camden Town, then walk to the Zoo.

activities.title: Hampton Court Palace Easter Lindt Gold Bunny Hunt
activities.description: Walk through the gardens, looking for elusive Lindt GOLD BUNNY statues peeking out from daffodils. There's chocolate prize at the end.
activities.whyItWorks: A classic Easter activity in a beautiful setting. Kids can explore the gardens and enjoy a chocolate treat.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Liverpool Street, then a train to Hampton Court.

activities.title: Visit a London City Farm
activities.description: See spring arrivals and enjoy a day out at a city farm.
activities.whyItWorks: A great way to see animals and learn about farming in the city. Perfect for a day out with a toddler.

transport.fromHackney: Varies depending on the farm. Check the farm's website for directions.

activities.title: Sky Garden Visit
activities.description: Visit the Sky Garden, a stunning indoor garden with panoramic views of the city.
activities.whyItWorks: A unique experience with great views. Kids will enjoy seeing the city from above.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Bank, then walk to the Sky Garden.
transport.tip: Be sure to book your tickets in advance.

activities.title: Kew Gardens - Octonauts Above and Beyond
activities.description: Join the Octonauts on an exciting outdoor mission at Kew Gardens. An interactive trail that invites young explorers to complete challenges, discover facts and learn how to protect our planet.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines a visit to the beautiful Kew Gardens with an interactive trail that is perfect for young children.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Richmond.

activities.title: Peppa Pig's egg-stra special Easter Afternoon Tea Bus Tour
activities.description: Every day this Easter a Peppa Pig themed afternoon tea will be run with special Easter travel cup and Easter Egg hunt activity on a London bus.
activities.whyItWorks: An afternoon tea on a bus with Peppa Pig theme with an easter egg hunt activity.

transport.fromHackney: Check departure point

activities.title: London Zoo's Zoonormous Eater Egg Hunt
activities.description: Kids are invited to explore London Zoo on a special Easter egg hunt, searching for six giant Easter eggs hidden around the park. Each egg reveals a secret letter and a riddle to solve and at the end there's a chocolate prize! In addition there is arts and crafts activities and Easter Bunny crowns.
activities.whyItWorks: Fun Easter themed day out with animals and a chocolate prize.

transport.fromHackney: Check website for details

activities.title: Easter Egg Hunt at Merchant Square
activities.description: Join the Marvellous Mouse Twins for a Whisker-Twitching Easter Adventure! Annual Easter Egg Hunt Coming Back to Merchant Square.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive Easter adventure with dress-up, storytelling, songs, games, and a thrilling quest to find the hidden eggs.

transport.fromHackney: Check website for details

activities.title: The Big Egg Hunt
activities.description: The Big Egg Hunt 2025 brings 100+ artist-designed eggs to London's landmarks. Explore, track eggs on The Big Egg Hunt app, and unlock surprises!
activities.whyItWorks: A city-wide trail featuring over 100 oversized, artist-decorated eggs hidden around central neighbourhoods and landmark buildings. Using a custom Big Egg Hunt app, your group can track the eggs, unlock digital clues and stories, and even compete for small prizes or photo challenges.

transport.fromHackney: Varies depending on location

activities.title: Willows Farm
activities.description: Family fun including Easter Egg Hunt, Easter Grotto, Easter arts and crafts and meeting cute animals including Easter bunnies. Plus Peter Rabbit shows and Jemima Puddle Duck activities.
activities.whyItWorks: Easter themed farm activities.

transport.fromHackney: Check website for details

activities.title: Lindt Gold Bunny Hunt at Hampton Court Palace
activities.description: Walk through the gardens, looking for elusive Lindt GOLD BUNNY statues peeking out from daffodils. There's chocolate prize at the end.
activities.whyItWorks: Easter themed chocolate hunt in the grounds of a palace.

transport.fromHackney: Check website for details

activities.title: Paddington Bear Experience
activities.description: An exciting immersive adventure through some of the most memorable locations from the Paddington stories.
activities.whyItWorks: Explore themed rooms and adventures themed around the paddington bear stories.

transport.fromHackney: Check website for details
